title: U_VERSION for Improving Locking Performance
text: U_VERSION for Improving Locking Performance Uniface supports a version mechanism which can improve performance by using a field called U_VERSION. U_VERSION is a one-byte field which you must assign to an entity for it to work. In other words, it is not automatically available. During update or delete operations, Uniface uses U_VERSION to ascertain whether a row of data has been changed by another process. If the value of U_VERSION has not changed, Uniface assumes that nothing else in the row has changed. Without U_VERSION, Uniface checks the value of every field in the row for changes. Implementing U_VERSION If you want to improve locking performance with a particular entity, assign a field to the entity with data type S and interface C1 and call this field U_VERSION. Make sure that you only use this definition, and that the field is assigned in the fixed-length part of the record. The best place to define this is immediately after the primary key. Never define U_VERSION as a key field of any sort. How U_VERSION Works Uniface records the version of a retrieved occurrence in U_VERSION. By checking only the values of U_VERSION in the database and in the component, Uniface recognizes immediately during a update and/or delete operation if the data in the database has been changed by someone else in the meantime. The more data an occurrence contains, the greater the performance improvement. Note: U_VERSION must be initialized to a value greater than ‘!’ (ASCII 33) and incremented to the next ASCII character in sequence. It cannot be a NULL value. However, after it is initialized, do not assign values to U_VERSION explicitly. To accomplish this version check, the value of U_VERSION is included in the where clause of update/delete for all field-level connectors, no matter what type of locking is used. Although the version check does not change the connector requests in paranoid locking situations, it does cause different connector requests to occur in cautious and optimistic locking situations. Cautious Locking If Uniface is unable to make the DBMS lock the relevant row in the DBMS when the lock instruction is issued, Uniface compares the occurrence in the component with the occurrence in the database. To do this without U_VERSION, Uniface has to fetch the whole occurrence, using connector request ‘F’, mode 1. (‘F’—for Fetch—is the actual request to the connector from the DBMS interface.) If there is a U_VERSION field, Uniface only has to compare the value of this field with the value of the stored field to see if the occurrence has changed in the database since the last read . The following table lists the connector requests for the lock and write ProcScript statements. Table 1. Cautious Locking—Connector Requests ProcScript instruction Connector requests performed lock Connector request: F, mode: 1 write Connector request: F, mode: 2 ($storetype=0) Connector request: U, mode: 0 Optimistic Locking With optimistic locking, the lock only occurs for the time it takes to write a row. The lock trigger is not even activated. Although this means that locks are present for a far shorter time, a lock (or stored modification) from another user stops the transaction. The U_VERSION field stops the lock conflict from occurring, as Uniface only attempts to lock, write and update if the value of the U_VERSION field is the same in the database as the value in the component. For many RDBMSs, only the update is required if both U_VERSION values are the same, thus minimizing I/O. The request for update is issued for the occurrence, and the value of U_VERSION is included in the where clause: if there is a difference, the update does not occur. This is not possible for most non-relational DBMSs and file management systems, because the update where functionality is not generally supported. The following table shows the connector requests which for the lock and write ProcScript statements. Table 2. Optimistic Locking—Connector Requests ProcScript instruction Connector requests without U_VERSION Connector requests with U_VERSION lock None None write Connector request: F, mode: 1 Connector request: U, mode: 0 ($storetype=0) Connector request: U, mode: 0 — Component Layout U_VERSION does not have to be actually painted on the Layout Editor; indeed, it is recommended not to do so. If defined, the U_VERSION field is automatically included in the field list for the entity. Related Links Benefits and Risks of U_VERSION U_VERSION is a special field that Uniface uses during update or delete operations to ascertain whether a row of data has been changed by another process. If the value of U_VERSION has not changed, Uniface assumes that nothing else in the row has changed. Without U_VERSION, Uniface checks the value of every field in the row for changes. Using a U_VERSION Field in a Server Page Data integrity can be maintained using the U_VERSION field in a web application. You can copy the U_VERSION field to a hidden non-database field that is sent to the browser. When handling a request, compare the hidden field with the U_VERSION to decide if the modified data can be written to the database. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
